name: Roasted Vegetable Pitas
recipeIngredient: ¼ teaspoon salt, or to taste
¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, or to taste
(do not use dried cilantro; if fresh is unavailable substitute fresh basil or flat-leaf parsley)
1 ½ cups cooked soybeans (one 15-ounce can -- drained and rinsed)
recipeInstructions:
Makes 4 servings
Roasting concentrates flavors without adding fat and lends a buttery-soft texture.
After roasting the peppers, position the oven rack about 6 inches from the broiler heating element. Pierce the eggplant several places with a fork; place it on a baking sheet. Turning once as it cooks, broil the eggplant for about 15 minutes, or until it is very tender when pierced with a fork. Set it aside to cool.
Move the oven rack to the center of the oven; preheat the oven to 350°F.
Whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, ¼ teaspoon of the salt, and pepper in a small bowl. Adjust the seasonings to taste.
When the eggplant has cooled, peel it and cut into 1-inch cubes. Toss the eggplant, cilantro, and lemon juice mixture in a medium bowl. Set aside.
Cut the peeled roasted red bell peppers lengthwise into ¼-inch-wide strips. Set aside.
Combine the soybeans, cumin, and remaining ¼ teaspoon salt in a small bowl.
Just before serving, wrap each pita bread in foil; place directly on the oven rack for about 5 minutes, or until softened. (Or put the unwrapped pitses in the microwave on high for about 45 seconds.) Use a serrated knife to cut each pita in half horizontally.
Place the 4 pita halves, rough sides up, on large salad plates. Evenly spread each pita round with the eggplant mixture: top with bell pepper strips and mounds of the bean mixture. Add a dollop of yogurt to each pit; sprinkle with the scallions and pepper.
